title: selaginella mortoniana
intro: Selaginella mortoniana, also known as resurrection fern or false maidenhair, is a type of evergreen perennial plant in the Selaginellaceae family, characterized by its ability to revive itself after drying out due to its unique water-storing properties. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which have been attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Selaginella mortoniana include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce oxidative stress, and improve cardiovascular health, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various conditions. Selaginella mortoniana can be prepared as a tea, extract, or capsule, and has also been used in traditional folk medicine in various cultures around the world.
